title: Sequence-Independent, Single-Primer Amplification of RNA Viruses
author: Gage Moreno
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bhk4j4yw

steps.title: Add 280µL cell-free liquid to 0.22µm centrifuge filter. Note
steps.instruction: Add 280µL cell-free liquid to 0.22µm centrifuge filter. Note: (add in 2x what you want to get out) Filter (0.22µm) Costar Catalog #8110

steps.title: Centrigue at 14,000 RPM for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrigue at 14,000 RPM for 5 minutes Centrifuge (Benchtop Centrifuge, Eppendorf, 5405000441, Any benchtop centrifuge will suffice, https://online-shop.eppendorf.us/US-en/Centrifugation-44533/Centrifuges-44534/Centrifuge-5425-PF-243560.html ) 14000 rpm, °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Transfer 140µL of filtered sample to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer 140µL of filtered sample to a new tube.

steps.title: Prepare Buffer AVL and linear polyacrylamide mix
steps.instruction: Prepare Buffer AVL and linear polyacrylamide mix. * For one sample, 560µL Buffer AVL + 5.6µL linear polyacrylamide Reagent Volume (µL) Linear Acrylamide (5 mg/ml) (1 ml Tube) Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM9520 QIAamp® Viral RNA Mini Qiagen Catalog #52906

steps.title: Pipet 560μL prepared Buffer AVL containing...
steps.instruction: Pipet 560μL prepared Buffer AVL containing linear polyacrylamide into a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. * Note: If the sample volume is larger than 140 μl, increase the amount of Buffer AVL–linear polyacrylamide proportionally (e.g., a 280 μl sample will require 1120 μl Buffer AVL–linear polyacrylamide) and use a larger tube.

steps.title: Add 140 μl plasma, serum, urine,...
steps.instruction: Add 140 μl plasma, serum, urine, cell-culture supernatant or cell-free body fluid to the Buffer AVL–LPA in the microcentrifuge tubes. Mix by pulse-vortexing for 15 s. 00:00:15 * Note: To ensure efficient lysis, it is essential that the sample is mixed thoroughly with Buffer AVL to yield a homogeneous solution. Frozen samples that have only been thawed once can also be used.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (15–25°C) for 10 min. * Note
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (15–25°C) for 10 min. * Note: Viral particle lysis is complete after lysis for 10 min at room temperature. Longer incubation times have no effect on the yield or quality of the purified RNA. 00:10:00

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the tube to remove...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the tube to remove drops from the inside of the lid.

steps.title: Add 560 μl ethanol (96–100%) to...
steps.instruction: Add 560 μl ethanol (96–100%) to each sample, and mix by pulse-vortexing for 15 s. After mixing, briefly centrifuge the tube to remove drops from inside the lid. 00:00:15 * Note: Use only ethanol, since other alcohols may result in reduced RNA yield and purity. Do not use denatured alcohol, which contains other substances such as methanol or methylethylketone. If the sample volume is greater than 140 μl, increase the amount of ethanol proportionally (e.g., a 280 μl sample will require 1120 μl ethanol). To ensure efficient binding, it is essential that the sample is mixed thoroughly with the ethanol to yield a homogeneous solution.

steps.title: Carefully apply 630 μl of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully apply 630 μl of the solution from step 8 to the QIAamp Mini column (in a 2 ml collection tube) without wetting the rim. Close the cap, and centrifuge at 6000 x g (8000 rpm) for 1 min. Place the QIAamp Mini column into a clean 2 ml collection tube, and discard the tube containing the filtrate. * Note: Close each spin column to avoid cross-contamination during centrifugation. * Note: Centrifugation is performed at 6000 x g (8000 rpm) to limit microcentrifuge noise. Centrifugation at full speed will not affect the yield or purity of the viral RNA. If the solution has not completely passed through the membrane, centrifuge again at a higher speed until all of the solution has passed through. 8000 rpm, °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and repeat step 9
steps.instruction: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and repeat step 9. If the sample volume was greater than 140 μl, repeat this step until all of the lysate has been loaded onto the spin column.

steps.title: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column,...
steps.instruction: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and add 500 μl Buffer AW2. Close the cap and centrifuge at full speed (20,000 x g; 14,000 rpm) for 3 min. Continue directly with step 14, or to eliminate possible Buffer AW2 carryover, perform step 13 and then continue. * Note: Residual Buffer AW2 in the eluate may cause problems in downstream applications. Some centrifuge rotors may vibrate upon deceleration, resulting in flow- through, containing Buffer AW2, contacting the QIAamp Mini column. 14000 rpm, °C, 3 minutes

steps.title: Recommended
steps.instruction: Recommended: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a new 2 ml collection tube (not provided), and discard the old collection tube with the filtrate. Centrifuge at full speed for 1 min. 14000 rpm, °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a clean 1
steps.instruction: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube (not provided). Discard the old collection tube containing the filtrate. Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column and add 30 μl DNase/RNase Free Water equilibrated to room temperature. Close the cap, and incubate at room temperature for 1 min.800

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6000 x g (8000 rpm) for 1 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6000 x g (8000 rpm) for 1 min. 8000 rpm, °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Transfer 30µL of eluted nucleic acid...
steps.instruction: Transfer 30µL of eluted nucleic acid to a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Create a master mix of TURBO DNase Mix
steps.instruction: Create a master mix of TURBO DNase Mix: Add 1 μL TURBO DNase (2 U) (for up to 10 μg RNA in a 50 μL reaction), 5µL TURBO DNase Buffer, and 14µL of H2O per sample. TURBO™ DNase (2 U/µL) Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM2238

steps.title: Add 20µL of TURBO DNase Mix to 30µL of sample
steps.instruction: Add 20µL of TURBO DNase Mix to 30µL of sample.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes. 37 °C 00:30:00 ThermoMixer (Benchtop Incubator, Eppendorf, 5382000023, Any heat block will suffice, https://online-shop.eppendorf.us/US-en/Temperature-Control-and-Mixing-44518/Instruments-44519/Eppendorf-ThermoMixerC-PF-19703.html , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/bcrsa.jpeg )

steps.title: Add 2 volumes RNA Binding buffer to each sample (ex
steps.instruction: Add 2 volumes RNA Binding buffer to each sample (ex. 100µL buffer + 50 µL sample) RNA Clean & Concentrator™-5 Zymo Research Catalog #R1015

steps.title: Add an equal amount of 100% ethanol and mix
steps.instruction: Add an equal amount of 100% ethanol and mix. (ex. 150µL ethanol)

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the Zymo-Spin...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the Zymo-Spin IC Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through * For samples >800 µL, Zymo-Spin columns can be reloaded 14000 rpm, °C, 30 seconds Centrifuge (Benchtop Centrifuge, Eppendorf, 5405000441, Any benchtop centrifuge will suffice, https://online-shop.eppendorf.us/US-en/Centrifugation-44533/Centrifuges-44534/Centrifuge-5425-PF-243560.html )

steps.title: Add 400 µL RNA Prep Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge at 10,000g– 16,000g for 30 seconds. Discard flow-through. 14000 rpm, °C, 30 seconds

steps.title: Add 700 µL RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge at 10,000g– 16,000g for 30 seconds. Discard flow-through. 14000 rpm, °C, 30 seconds

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000g– 16,000g for 2...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000g– 16,000g for 2 minutes to ensure complete removal of the wash buffer. Transfer the column carefully into an RNase free tube (Not provided in RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit). 14000 rpm, °C, 2 minutes

steps.title: Add 8-10 µL Nuclease-Free water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 8-10 µL Nuclease-Free water directly to the column matrix and centrifuge at 10,000g– 16,000g for 30 seconds. The eluted RNA can be used immediately or stored at -70°C.

steps.title: Make a working stock of your primer A. Stock
steps.instruction: Make a working stock of your primer A. Stock: 100pmol/1µL. Add 4µL of stock + 6µL H2O. You now have a 10µL of a 40pmol/µL working stock.

steps.title: Add 1 µL Primer A working...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL Primer A working stock to 4 µL extracted RNA. Heat to 65°C for 5 minutes in a thermocycler and let cool at 4°C for 5 minutes. SimpliAmp Thermal Cycler (PCR, Applied Biosystems, A24811, Any standard PCR thermocycler will suffice, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A24811 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9df3sqe.jpeg )

steps.title: While reaction is on the thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: While reaction is on the thermocycler, make a master mix for 1 reaction (scale up as needed) consisting of 2 μL 5X RT buffer, 1 μL 10 mM dNTP, 1 μL water, 0.5 μL 0.1M DTT, and 0.5 μL SSIV RT.

steps.title: Add 5 µL master mix to reaction
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL master mix to reaction. Incubate at 50°C for 10 minutes. 50 °C 00:10:00

steps.title: While reaction is on the thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: While reaction is on the thermocycler, make a master mix for 1 reaction (scale up as needed) consisting of 1 μL 5X Sequenase buffer, 3.8 μL water, and 0.15 μL Sequenase.

steps.title: After 10 minute incubation, Add 5...
steps.instruction: After 10 minute incubation, Add 5 µL of Sequenase Mix #1 to the reaction.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 8 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 8 min. 37 °C 00:08:00

steps.title: While reaction is on the thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: While reaction is on the thermocycler, make a master mix for 1 reaction (scale up as needed) consisting of 0.45 μL Sequenase dilution buffer, and 0.15 μL Sequenase.

steps.title: After 8 min incubation, add 0
steps.instruction: After 8 min incubation, add 0.6µL of Sequenase Mix #2 to the reaction.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 8 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 8 min. 37 °C 00:08:00

steps.title: After 8 min incubation, add 1µL...
steps.instruction: After 8 min incubation, add 1µL of RNase H to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate reaction at 37°C for 20 min
steps.instruction: Incubate reaction at 37°C for 20 min. 37 °C 00:20:00 * Round A is now complete and samples can be stored at -20°C

steps.title: Make a master mix for 1...
steps.instruction: Make a master mix for 1 reaction (scale up as needed) consisting of 5 µL AccuTaq LA 10x Buffer, 2.5 µL dNTP mix, 1µL DMSO, 0.5 µL AccuTaq LA DNA Polymerase, 35 µL nuclease free water, and 1 µL Primer B. *Do not dilute Primer B

steps.title: Add 5 µL of product from...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of product from SISPA Round A to 45 µL master mix.

steps.title: Run PCR with the following conditions...
steps.instruction: Run PCR with the following conditions 98°C for 30s, followed by 30 cycles of 94°C for 15 s, 50°C for 20 s, and 68°C for 2 min, a final step of 68°C for 10 min, and then a 4°C hold until you’re ready for the cleanup. * Can freeze in the -20°C until ready SimpliAmp Thermal Cycler (PCR, Applied Biosystems, A24811, Any standard PCR thermocycler will suffice, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A24811 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9df3sqe.jpeg )

steps.title: Amplified cDNA was purified using a...
steps.instruction: Amplified cDNA was purified using a 1:1 ratio of AMPure XP beads. Add 50µL of resupsended AMPure XP beads to 50µL SISPA product.

steps.title: Incubate at RT for 10 minutes Room temperature 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at RT for 10 minutes Room temperature 00:10:00

steps.title: Spin down briefly Mini-centrifuge (Centrifuge, Fisher,...
steps.instruction: Spin down briefly Mini-centrifuge (Centrifuge, Fisher, S67601B, Any standard mini centrifuge with adapters for different tube sizes will suffice, https://www.fishersci.com/shop/products/fisherbrand-standard-mini-centrifuge-standard-mini-centrifuge/s67601b , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9dh3sqe.jpeg )

steps.title: Place on magnet
steps.instruction: Place on magnet. Remove and discard supernatant once solution turns clear. Be sure not to disturb beads. Magnetic Stand (Magnetic Stand, Thermo Scientific, MR02, Any magnetic rack that fits your tubes will suffice., https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/MR02 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9e53sqe.jpeg )

steps.title: Wash with 200µL of 70% EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash with 200µL of 70% EtOH.

steps.title: Remove EtOH without disturbing beads
steps.instruction: Remove EtOH without disturbing beads.

steps.title: Repeat wash
steps.instruction: Repeat wash. Remove EtOH without disturbing beads.

steps.title: Remove samples from magnetic rack and spin down briefly
steps.instruction: Remove samples from magnetic rack and spin down briefly.

steps.title: Remove residual ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove residual ethanol

steps.title: Let air dry briefly - Do not overdry bead pellet
steps.instruction: Let air dry briefly - Do not overdry bead pellet

steps.title: Resuspend sample in 50µL of H2O
steps.instruction: Resuspend sample in 50µL of H2O.

steps.title: Incubate samples at RT for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at RT for 5 minutes. Room temperature 00:05:00

steps.title: Transfer tubes to magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes to magnetic rack. Transfer 48µL of eluted product to a new tube.

steps.title: Continue onto library prep protocol of your choice
steps.instruction: Continue onto library prep protocol of your choice.
